members.name: GetAndroidSurface
members.summary: Returns a [JavaObject] representing an [code]android.view.Surface[/code] if [member use_android_surface] is enabled and OpenXR has created the surface. Otherwise, this will return [code]null[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] The surface can only be created during an active OpenXR session. So, if [member use_android_surface] is enabled outside of an OpenXR session, it won't be created until a new session fully starts.

members.name: IntersectsRay
members.signature: public Vector2 IntersectsRay(Vector3 origin, Vector3 direction)
members.summary: Returns UV coordinates where the given ray intersects with the composition layer. [param origin] and [param direction] must be in global space. Returns [code]Vector2(-1.0, -1.0)[/code] if the ray doesn't intersect.

members.name: IsNativelySupported
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the OpenXR runtime natively supports this composition layer type. [b]Note:[/b] This will only return an accurate result after the OpenXR session has started.

members.name: AlphaBlend
members.summary: Enables the blending the layer using its alpha channel. Can be combined with [member Viewport.transparent_bg] to give the layer a transparent background.

members.name: AndroidSurfaceSize
members.summary: The size of the Android surface to create if [member use_android_surface] is enabled.

members.name: EnableHolePunch
members.summary: Enables a technique called "hole punching", which allows putting the composition layer behind the main projection layer (i.e. setting [member sort_order] to a negative value) while "punching a hole" through everything rendered by Godot so that the layer is still visible. This can be used to create the illusion that the composition layer exists in the same 3D space as everything rendered by Godot, allowing objects to appear to pass both behind or in front of the composition layer.

members.name: EyeVisibility
members.summary: The eye(s) the composition layer is visible to. [b]Note:[/b] Not all composition layer types or runtimes support restricting visibility to a single eye.

members.name: LayerViewport
members.summary: The [SubViewport] to render on the composition layer.

members.name: ProtectedContent
members.summary: If enabled, the OpenXR swapchain will be created with the [code]XR_SWAPCHAIN_CREATE_PROTECTED_CONTENT_BIT[/code] flag, which will protect its contents from CPU access. When used with an Android Surface, this may allow DRM content to be presented, and will only take effect when the Surface is first created; later changes to this property will have no effect.

members.name: SortOrder
members.summary: The sort order for this composition layer. Higher numbers will be shown in front of lower numbers. [b]Note:[/b] This will have no effect if a fallback mesh is being used.

members.name: SwapchainStateAlphaSwizzle
members.summary: The swizzle value for the alpha channel of the swapchain state. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: SwapchainStateBlueSwizzle
members.summary: The swizzle value for the blue channel of the swapchain state. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: SwapchainStateBorderColor
members.summary: The border color of the swapchain state that is used when the wrap mode clamps to the border. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: SwapchainStateGreenSwizzle
members.summary: The swizzle value for the green channel of the swapchain state. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: SwapchainStateHorizontalWrap
members.summary: The horizontal wrap mode of the swapchain state. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: SwapchainStateMagFilter
members.summary: The magnification filter of the swapchain state. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: SwapchainStateMaxAnisotropy
members.summary: The max anisotropy of the swapchain state. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: SwapchainStateMinFilter
members.summary: The minification filter of the swapchain state. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: SwapchainStateMipmapMode
members.summary: The mipmap mode of the swapchain state. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: SwapchainStateRedSwizzle
members.summary: The swizzle value for the red channel of the swapchain state. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: SwapchainStateVerticalWrap
members.summary: The vertical wrap mode of the swapchain state. [b]Note:[/b] This property only has an effect on devices that support the OpenXR XR_FB_swapchain_update_state OpenGLES/Vulkan extensions.

members.name: UseAndroidSurface
members.summary: If enabled, an Android surface will be created (with the dimensions from [member android_surface_size]) which will provide the 2D content for the composition layer, rather than using [member layer_viewport]. See [method get_android_surface] for information about how to get the surface so that your application can draw to it. [b]Note:[/b] This will only work in Android builds.

values.name: EYE_VISIBILITY_BOTH
values.summary: The layer is visible to both the left and right eyes.

values.name: EYE_VISIBILITY_LEFT
values.summary: The layer is visible only to the left eye.

values.name: EYE_VISIBILITY_RIGHT
values.summary: The layer is visible only to the right eye.

values.name: FILTER_CUBIC
values.summary: Perform cubic filtering when sampling the texture.

values.name: FILTER_LINEAR
values.summary: Perform linear filtering when sampling the texture.

values.name: FILTER_NEAREST
values.summary: Perform nearest-neighbor filtering when sampling the texture.

values.name: MIPMAP_MODE_DISABLED
values.summary: Disable mipmapping. [b]Note:[/b] Mipmapping can only be disabled in the Compatibility renderer.

values.name: MIPMAP_MODE_LINEAR
values.summary: Use linear interpolation of the two mipmaps of the nearest resolution.

values.name: MIPMAP_MODE_NEAREST
values.summary: Use the mipmap of the nearest resolution.

values.name: SWIZZLE_ALPHA
values.summary: Maps a color channel to the value of the alpha channel.

values.name: SWIZZLE_BLUE
values.summary: Maps a color channel to the value of the blue channel.

values.name: SWIZZLE_GREEN
values.summary: Maps a color channel to the value of the green channel.

values.name: SWIZZLE_ONE
values.summary: Maps a color channel to the value of one.

values.name: SWIZZLE_RED
values.summary: Maps a color channel to the value of the red channel.

values.name: SWIZZLE_ZERO
values.summary: Maps a color channel to the value of zero.

values.name: WRAP_CLAMP_TO_BORDER
values.summary: Clamp the texture to its specified border color.

values.name: WRAP_CLAMP_TO_EDGE
values.summary: Clamp the texture to its edge color.

values.name: WRAP_MIRRORED_REPEAT
values.summary: Repeat the texture infinitely, mirroring it on each repeat.

values.name: WRAP_MIRROR_CLAMP_TO_EDGE
values.summary: Mirror the texture once and then clamp the texture to its edge color. [b]Note:[/b] This wrap mode is not available in the Compatibility renderer.

values.name: WRAP_REPEAT
values.summary: Repeat the texture infinitely.
